title: rhus kearneyi subsp. virginum
intro: Rhus kearneyi subsp. virginum, also known as the Kearney's sumac or Virgin's sumac, is a shrub species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rhus kearneyi subsp. virginum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
